topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on the tablet screen, a string of cryptic messages from an account the teenager, Leo, had never mentioned. My stomach clenched, a tight knot forming beneath my ribs. I clicked it, and the feed was a curated highlight reel of their life, filled with smiling faces and impossibly perfect sunsets. A flush crept up my neck, warm and prickly. I leaned closer to the screen, my jaw tightening. "Leo," I started, my voice unnaturally level, "what is this? I didn't know you had another profile." Leo, who was ostensibly doing homework across the room, slowly lowered his textbook, his eyes flickering toward me with a practiced, evasive glance. The silence stretched, thick and uncomfortable, punctuated only by the faint hum of the air conditioning. I felt a distinct hardening in my chest, a sudden need to interrogate every pixel of this hidden world.

[story 2]

I found the login details tucked away in a misplaced browser bookmark, a digital breadcrumb leading directly into the unexpected. When the profile loaded, it was a stark contrast to the careful, muted existence I thought I knew. It was vibrant, loud, and undeniably… performative. A slow, simmering heat rose in my cheeks. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the hardwood floor. "This is completely inappropriate," I stated, my tone sharper than intended, laced with a distinct edge of disbelief. Leo jumped, startled, dropping his phone. He looked down at his lap, his shoulders hunched, avoiding my gaze entirely. I crossed my arms tightly across my chest, the tension in my arms radiating outward. I wanted to demand an explanation, to see the reality behind this carefully constructed facade, but the words caught in my throat, replaced by a stiff, rigid posture.

[story 3]

It was the accidental discovery—a forgotten password in a shared device—that brought the whole thing crashing down. Peering at the stream of posts, I felt a profound sense of being excluded, as if I had stumbled upon a secret society. A low, frustrated sound escaped my lips. "You've been keeping this hidden?" I asked, my voice dangerously quiet, the volume low but the intent razor-sharp. Leo finally looked up, his expression a mixture of alarm and something akin to resignation. He didn't deny it, just offered a weak shrug. I took a deliberate step back, my gaze sweeping over the screen as if physically repulsed by the curated perfection. My hands clenched into fists at my sides; the feeling was a hot, burning pressure behind my eyes, demanding acknowledgment of the deception.
